content.content:
1→# Domain Model Reference
     2→
     3→This document defines the domain entities, data structures, and business rules for the MMS System.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Fleet Composition
     8→
     9→### Demo Implementation (Current)
    10→
    11→| Property | Value | Notes |
    12→|----------|-------|-------|
    13→| Total Trains | 50 | Simplified for demo |
    14→| Time Range | 10 years | Mock data generator |
    15→| Tasks per Train | ~120/year | Varies by maintenance type |
    16→| Total Tasks | ~60,000 | Across all trains/years |
    17→
    18→### Full System (From RFQ)
    19→
    20→| Type | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot |
    21→|------|--------|-------|------|-------|
    22→| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya |
    23→| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali |
    24→| **Total** | | **128** | | |
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Maintenance Types
    29→
    30→| Level | Name | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Est. Cost |
    31→|-------|------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|-----------|
    32→| A | A-Check | 10,000 | ±2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |
    33→| B | B-Check | 50,000 | ±5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |
    34→| C | C-Check | 200,000 | ±20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |
    35→| D | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | ±50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |
    36→
    37→### Maintenance Triggers
    38→
    39→1. **Mileage-based** (primary): Triggered when train reaches interval threshold
    40→2. **Time-based** (secondary): Calendar-based inspections
    41→3. **Condition-based** (future): Based on sensor data and predictive analytics
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## Depot Configuration
    46→
    47→| Depot ID | Name | Tracks | Weekly Man-Hours | Serves |
    48→|----------|------|--------|------------------|--------|
    49→| depot-red | Al Rashidiya | 5 | 2,800 | Alstom (Red Line) |
    50→| depot-green | Jebel Ali | 4 | 2,400 | Kinki Sharyo (Green Line) |
    51→
    52→### Resource Constraints
    53→
    54→```
    55→Weekly Man-Hours: 5,200 (combined)
    56→Monthly Man-Hours: 20,800
    57→Total Maintenance Tracks: 9
    58→Track Capacity/Day: 2 trains per track
    59→```
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Fleet Characteristics
    64→
    65→### Alstom (ALT) Fleet
    66→- **Count**: 79 trains
    67→- **Cars per train**: 5
    68→- **Base mileage**: ~450,000 - 800,000 km
    69→- **Daily average**: 420-475 km/day
    70→- **Assigned depot**: Al Rashidiya
    71→
    72→### Kinki Sharyo (KNK) Fleet
    73→- **Count**: 49 trains (newer fleet)
    74→- **Cars per train**: 5
    75→- **Base mileage**: ~320,000 - 600,000 km
    76→- **Daily average**: 400-454 km/day
    77→- **Assigned depot**: Jebel Ali
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→## TypeScript Interfaces
    82→
    83→These interfaces are implemented in `packages/domain/src/entities/`.
    84→
    85→### Train Entity
    86→
    87→```typescript
    88→interface Train {
    89→  id: string;                    // "TRAIN-001", "TRAIN-050"
    90→  name: string;                  // Display name
    91→  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
    92→  
    93→  // Full system would include:
    94→  // assetNumber: string;
    95→  // assetType: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';
    96→  // carsCount: number;
    97→  // currentMileageKm: number;
    98→  // avgDailyMileageKm: number;
    99→  // lastMaintenanceDate: string;
   100→  // status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';
   101→  // lastMaintenanceKm: Record<MaintenanceLevel, number>;
   102→}
   103→```
   104→
   105→### Task Entity
   106→
   107→```typescript
   108→interface Task {
   109→  id: string;                    // Unique task identifier
   110→  trainId: string;               // Reference to train
   111→  startDate: Date;               // Task start
   112→  endDate: Date;                 // Task end
   113→  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
   114→  conflict: boolean;             // Has scheduling conflict
   115→  dependencies: string[];        // IDs of dependent tasks
   116→  
   117→  // Full system would include:
   118→  // level: 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';
   119→  // mileageAtStart: number;
   120→  // manHoursRequired: number;
   121→  // tracksRequired: number;
   122→  // estimatedCost: number;
   123→  // status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'overdue';
   124→}
   125→```
   126→
   127→### Conflict Entity
   128→
   129→```typescript
   130→interface Conflict {
   131→  id: string;
   132→  taskIds: [string, string];     // The two conflicting tasks
   133→  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
   134→  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
   135→  
   136→  // Full system would include:
   137→  // description: string;
   138→  // suggestedResolution: string;
   139→  // autoResolvable: boolean;
   140→}
   141→```
   142→
   143→### Depot Entity (Future)
   144→
   145→```typescript
   146→interface Depot {
   147→  id: string;
   148→  name: string;
   149→  assetTypes: ('ALSTOM' | 'KINKI')[];
   150→  trackCount: number;
   151→  weeklyManHours: number;
   152→  location: { lat: number; lng: number };
   153→}
   154→```
   155→
   156→### Resource Constraints (Future)
   157→
   158→```typescript
   159→interface ResourceConstraints {
   160→  weeklyManHours: number;
   161→  monthlyManHours: number;
   162→  maintenanceTracks: number;
   163→  trackCapacityPerDay: number;
   164→  depots: Depot[];
   165→}
   166→```
   167→
   168→---
   169→
   170→## Data Generation
   171→
   172→### Mock Data Generator
   173→
   174→The demo uses `packages/domain/src/generators/mock-data.ts` to create:
   175→
   176→1. **50 Trains** with unique IDs
   177→2. **10 Years of Tasks** per train
   178→3. **Intentional Conflicts** (overlapping dates)
   179→4. **Dependencies** between tasks
   180→
   181→### Generation Algorithm
   182→
   183→```typescript
   184→function generateMockData(): Dataset {
   185→  const trains = generateTrains(50);
   186→  const tasks = trains.flatMap(train => 
   187→    generateTasksForTrain(train, 10) // 10 years
   188→  );
   189→  const conflicts = detectConflicts(tasks);
   190→  
   191→  return { trains, tasks, conflicts };
   192→}
   193→```
   194→
   195→### Conflict Injection
   196→
   197→To test the conflict visualization in MicroLayer, the generator intentionally creates:
   198→- 5-10% of tasks with overlapping dates
   199→- Resource conflicts (same track, same time)
   200→- Dependency violations
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→## Coordinate System
   205→
   206→### Canvas Coordinates
   207→
   208→The visualization uses a world-space coordinate system:
   209→
   210→| Axis | Represents | Scale |
   211→|------|-----------|-------|
   212→| X | Time | PX_PER_DAY = 10 pixels |
   213→| Y | Train Index | PX_PER_TRAIN = 24 pixels |
   214→| Z | Layer depth | 0 = front, -1 = back |
   215→
   216→### Conversion Functions
   217→
   218→```typescript
   219→// Time to X position
   220→function dateToX(date: Date, startDate: Date): number {
   221→  const days = differenceInDays(date, startDate);
   222→  return days * PX_PER_DAY;
   223→}
   224→
   225→// Train index to Y position
   226→function trainIndexToY(index: number): number {
   227→  return index * PX_PER_TRAIN;
   228→}
   229→
   230→// Screen to world coordinates
   231→function screenToWorld(screenX: number, screenY: number, camera: Camera): Vector3 {
   232→  // ... projection math
   233→}
   234→```
   235→
   236→---
   237→
   238→## Heatmap Data Encoding
   239→
   240→For the MacroLayer (shader), train occupancy is encoded into a DataTexture:
   241→
   242→```typescript
   243→// Texture dimensions
   244→const TEXTURE_WIDTH = 4096;  // Days (≈11 years at 1px/day)
   245→const TEXTURE_HEIGHT = 50;   // Trains
   246→
   247→// Pixel encoding
   248→// R channel: Occupancy level (0.0 - 1.0)
   249→// G channel: Conflict flag (0.0 or 1.0)
   250→// B channel: Reserved
   251→// A channel: Always 1.0
   252→```
   253→
   254→---
   255→
   256→## Business Rules
   257→
   258→### Mileage Management
   259→
   260→1. **Overrun Risk**: Train within 5% of maintenance interval → Yellow warning
   261→2. **Overrun**: Train exceeds interval → Red alert, must schedule immediately
   262→3. **Underrun**: Train significantly below target mileage → Efficiency warning
   263→
   264→### Scheduling Constraints
   265→
   266→1. **Track Availability**: Max 2 trains per track per day
   267→2. **Man-Hours**: Cannot exceed weekly depot capacity
   268→3. **Dependencies**: Child tasks cannot start before parent completes
   269→4. **Tolerance Window**: Tasks must be scheduled within ±tolerance of ideal date
   270→
   271→### Conflict Resolution Priority
   272→
   273→1. Safety-critical (D-Overhaul) → Highest priority
   274→2. Regulatory compliance (C-Check) → High priority
   275→3. Preventive maintenance (A/B-Check) → Normal priority
   276→4. Optimization (equalizing) → Low priority
   277→
   278→---
   279→
   280→## Future Enhancements
   281→
   282→### Phase 1 (Current Demo)
   283→- ✅ Basic entities (Train, Task, Conflict)
   284→- ✅ Mock data generation
   285→- ✅ Visualization layers
   286→
   287→### Phase 2 (Next)
   288→- [ ] Real mileage integration
   289→- [ ] Depot and resource modeling
   290→- [ ] Cost calculations
   291→
   292→### Phase 3 (Future)
   293→- [ ] IBM Maximo integration
   294→- [ ] Predictive maintenance
   295→- [ ] What-If simulation engine
   296→

     1→# Domain Model Reference
     2→
     3→This document defines the domain entities, data structures, and business rules for the MMS System.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Fleet Composition
     8→
     9→### Demo Implementation (Current)
    10→
    11→| Property | Value | Notes |
    12→|----------|-------|-------|
    13→| Total Trains | 50 | Simplified for demo |
    14→| Time Range | 10 years | Mock data generator |
    15→| Tasks per Train | ~120/year | Varies by maintenance type |
    16→| Total Tasks | ~60,000 | Across all trains/years |
    17→
    18→### Full System (From RFQ)
    19→
    20→| Type | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot |
    21→|------|--------|-------|------|-------|
    22→| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya |
    23→| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali |
    24→| **Total** | | **128** | | |
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Maintenance Types
    29→
    30→| Level | Name | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Est. Cost |
    31→|-------|------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|-----------|
    32→| A | A-Check | 10,000 | ±2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |
    33→| B | B-Check | 50,000 | ±5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |
    34→| C | C-Check | 200,000 | ±20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |
    35→| D | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | ±50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |
    36→
    37→### Maintenance Triggers
    38→
    39→1. **Mileage-based** (primary): Triggered when train reaches interval threshold
    40→2. **Time-based** (secondary): Calendar-based inspections
    41→3. **Condition-based** (future): Based on sensor data and predictive analytics
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## Depot Configuration
    46→
    47→| Depot ID | Name | Tracks | Weekly Man-Hours | Serves |
    48→|----------|------|--------|------------------|--------|
    49→| depot-red | Al Rashidiya | 5 | 2,800 | Alstom (Red Line) |
    50→| depot-green | Jebel Ali | 4 | 2,400 | Kinki Sharyo (Green Line) |
    51→
    52→### Resource Constraints
    53→
    54→```
    55→Weekly Man-Hours: 5,200 (combined)
    56→Monthly Man-Hours: 20,800
    57→Total Maintenance Tracks: 9
    58→Track Capacity/Day: 2 trains per track
    59→```
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Fleet Characteristics
    64→
    65→### Alstom (ALT) Fleet
    66→- **Count**: 79 trains
    67→- **Cars per train**: 5
    68→- **Base mileage**: ~450,000 - 800,000 km
    69→- **Daily average**: 420-475 km/day
    70→- **Assigned depot**: Al Rashidiya
    71→
    72→### Kinki Sharyo (KNK) Fleet
    73→- **Count**: 49 trains (newer fleet)
    74→- **Cars per train**: 5
    75→- **Base mileage**: ~320,000 - 600,000 km
    76→- **Daily average**: 400-454 km/day
    77→- **Assigned depot**: Jebel Ali
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→## TypeScript Interfaces
    82→
    83→Implementation location: `apps/canvas/src/data/`
    84→
    85→### Architecture: Persistence vs Rendering
    86→
    87→Data flows through two distinct layers:
    88→
    89→```
    90→┌─────────────────┐    parse/     ┌─────────────────┐    compute    ┌─────────────────┐
    91→│   Persistent    │  ──────────>  │     Runtime     │  ──────────>  │    Rendering    │
    92→│   (JSON/API)    │               │   (in memory)   │               │    (canvas)     │
    93→│                 │               │                 │               │                 │
    94→│ startDate:      │               │ (Date objects   │               │ startDay: 74    │
    95→│ "2024-03-15"    │               │  for logic)     │               │ trainIndex: 5   │
    96→└─────────────────┘               └─────────────────┘               └─────────────────┘
    97→```
    98→
    99→**Why this separation?**
   100→- `Date` objects cannot be serialized to JSON (become `{}`)
   101→- Canvas needs numeric offsets for position calculations
   102→- Domain logic needs semantic dates for business rules
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→### Domain Types (Persistent/Serializable)
   107→
   108→These types can be saved to JSON, sent via API, or stored in localStorage.
   109→
   110→```typescript
   111→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→// ENUMS & CONSTANTS
   113→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   114→
   115→type AssetType = 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';
   116→type TaskType = 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
   117→type MaintenanceLevel = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';
   118→
   119→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   120→// TRAIN (Persistent)
   121→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   122→
   123→interface Train {
   124→  id: string;              // "TRAIN-001" or "ALT-001"
   125→  name: string;            // Display name
   126→  assetType: AssetType;    // Fleet type
   127→}
   128→
   129→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   130→// TASK (Persistent)
   131→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   132→
   133→interface Task {
   134→  id: string;              // Unique identifier
   135→  trainId: string;         // Reference to Train.id
   136→  startDate: string;       // ISO 8601 date: "2024-03-15"
   137→  endDate: string;         // ISO 8601 date: "2024-03-20"
   138→  type: TaskType;          // Visual category
   139→  hasConflict: boolean;    // Conflict indicator
   140→}
   141→
   142→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   143→// DATASET METADATA (Persistent)
   144→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   145→
   146→interface DatasetMeta {
   147→  startDate: string;       // ISO 8601: "2024-01-01"
   148→  trainCount: number;      // Number of trains
   149→  years: number;           // Time range in years
   150→}
   151→
   152→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   153→// GENERATED DATA (Complete dataset)
   154→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   155→
   156→interface GeneratedData {
   157→  trains: Train[];
   158→  tasks: Task[];
   159→  meta: DatasetMeta;
   160→}
   161→```
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→### Rendering Types (Runtime/Calculated)
   166→
   167→These types are computed when data loads and used by the canvas. Never persisted.
   168→
   169→```typescript
   170→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   171→// TASK RENDER (Calculated for canvas)
   172→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   173→
   174→type TaskColor = 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';
   175→
   176→interface TaskRender {
   177→  id: string;              // Same as Task.id
   178→  trainIndex: number;      // Y position (0-based index in trains array)
   179→  startDay: number;        // X position (days since meta.startDate)
   180→  duration: number;        // Width (days)
   181→  color: TaskColor;        // Derived from Task.type
   182→  hasConflict: boolean;    // Passthrough from Task
   183→  type: TaskType;          // For label display in MicroLayer
   184→}
   185→
   186→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   187→// MAPPINGS
   188→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   189→
   190→const TYPE_TO_COLOR: Record<TaskType, TaskColor> = {
   191→  maintenance: 'blue',
   192→  overhaul: 'orange',
   193→  inspection: 'gray',
   194→};
   195→```
   196→
   197→---
   198→
   199→### Canvas Configuration
   200→
   201→```typescript
   202→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   203→// VIEW CONFIG (Visual settings)
   204→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   205→
   206→interface ViewConfig {
   207→  pxPerDay: number;        // Pixels per day (X scale)
   208→  pxPerTrain: number;      // Pixels per train row (Y scale)
   209→  minZoom: number;         // Minimum zoom level
   210→  maxZoom: number;         // Maximum zoom level
   211→  zoomSensitivity: number; // Scroll sensitivity
   212→  lodThresholds: {
   213→    macro: number;         // Below this → MACRO view
   214→    micro: number;         // Above this → MICRO view
   215→  };
   216→}
   217→
   218→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   219→// VIEWPORT STATE (Runtime)
   220→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   221→
   222→type LODLevel = 'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO';
   223→
   224→interface ViewportState {
   225→  zoom: number;
   226→  panX: number;
   227→  panY: number;
   228→  lod: LODLevel;
   229→}
   230→
   231→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   232→// DEFAULT VALUES
   233→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   234→
   235→const DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG: ViewConfig = {
   236→  pxPerDay: 10,
   237→  pxPerTrain: 24,
   238→  minZoom: 0.1,
   239→  maxZoom: 5,
   240→  zoomSensitivity: 0.002,
   241→  lodThresholds: {
   242→    macro: 0.5,
   243→    micro: 2.0,
   244→  },
   245→};
   246→
   247→const DEFAULT_DATASET_META: DatasetMeta = {
   248→  startDate: '2024-01-01',
   249→  trainCount: 50,
   250→  years: 10,
   251→};
   252→```
   253→
   254→---
   255→
   256→### Future Extensions (Optional Fields)
   257→
   258→These fields can be added without breaking existing code:
   259→
   260→```typescript
   261→// Extended Train (future)
   262→interface TrainExtended extends Train {
   263→  carsCount?: number;
   264→  currentMileageKm?: number;
   265→  avgDailyMileageKm?: number;
   266→  status?: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';
   267→}
   268→
   269→// Extended Task (future)
   270→interface TaskExtended extends Task {
   271→  maintenanceLevel?: MaintenanceLevel;  // A/B/C/D
   272→  manHours?: number;
   273→  estimatedCost?: number;
   274→  dependencies?: string[];              // Predecessor task IDs
   275→  status?: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed';
   276→}
   277→
   278→// Conflict as separate entity (future)
   279→interface Conflict {
   280→  id: string;
   281→  taskIds: [string, string];
   282→  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
   283→  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
   284→  description?: string;
   285→}
   286→
   287→// Depot (future)
   288→interface Depot {
   289→  id: string;
   290→  name: string;
   291→  assetTypes: AssetType[];
   292→  trackCount: number;
   293→  weeklyManHours: number;
   294→}
   295→```
   296→
   297→---
   298→
   299→## Data Generation
   300→
   301→### Mock Data Generator
   302→
   303→The demo uses `packages/domain/src/generators/mock-data.ts` to create:
   304→
   305→1. **50 Trains** with unique IDs
   306→2. **10 Years of Tasks** per train
   307→3. **Intentional Conflicts** (overlapping dates)
   308→4. **Dependencies** between tasks
   309→
   310→### Generation Algorithm
   311→
   312→```typescript
   313→function generateMockData(): Dataset {
   314→  const trains = generateTrains(50);
   315→  const tasks = trains.flatMap(train => 
   316→    generateTasksForTrain(train, 10) // 10 years
   317→  );
   318→  const conflicts = detectConflicts(tasks);
   319→  
   320→  return { trains, tasks, conflicts };
   321→}
   322→```
   323→
   324→### Conflict Injection
   325→
   326→To test the conflict visualization in MicroLayer, the generator intentionally creates:
   327→- 5-10% of tasks with overlapping dates
   328→- Resource conflicts (same track, same time)
   329→- Dependency violations
   330→
   331→---
   332→
   333→## Coordinate System
   334→
   335→### Canvas Coordinates
   336→
   337→The visualization uses a world-space coordinate system:
   338→
   339→| Axis | Represents | Scale |
   340→|------|-----------|-------|
   341→| X | Time | PX_PER_DAY = 10 pixels |
   342→| Y | Train Index | PX_PER_TRAIN = 24 pixels |
   343→| Z | Layer depth | 0 = front, -1 = back |
   344→
   345→### Conversion Functions
   346→
   347→```typescript
   348→// Time to X position
   349→function dateToX(date: Date, startDate: Date): number {
   350→  const days = differenceInDays(date, startDate);
   351→  return days * PX_PER_DAY;
   352→}
   353→
   354→// Train index to Y position
   355→function trainIndexToY(index: number): number {
   356→  return index * PX_PER_TRAIN;
   357→}
   358→
   359→// Screen to world coordinates
   360→function screenToWorld(screenX: number, screenY: number, camera: Camera): Vector3 {
   361→  // ... projection math
   362→}
   363→```
   364→
   365→---
   366→
   367→## Heatmap Data Encoding
   368→
   369→For the MacroLayer (shader), train occupancy is encoded into a DataTexture:
   370→
   371→```typescript
   372→// Texture dimensions
   373→const TEXTURE_WIDTH = 4096;  // Days (≈11 years at 1px/day)
   374→const TEXTURE_HEIGHT = 50;   // Trains
   375→
   376→// Pixel encoding
   377→// R channel: Occupancy level (0.0 - 1.0)
   378→// G channel: Conflict flag (0.0 or 1.0)
   379→// B channel: Reserved
   380→// A channel: Always 1.0
   381→```
   382→
   383→---
   384→
   385→## Business Rules
   386→
   387→### Mileage Management
   388→
   389→1. **Overrun Risk**: Train within 5% of maintenance interval → Yellow warning
   390→2. **Overrun**: Train exceeds interval → Red alert, must schedule immediately
   391→3. **Underrun**: Train significantly below target mileage → Efficiency warning
   392→
   393→### Scheduling Constraints
   394→
   395→1. **Track Availability**: Max 2 trains per track per day
   396→2. **Man-Hours**: Cannot exceed weekly depot capacity
   397→3. **Dependencies**: Child tasks cannot start before parent completes
   398→4. **Tolerance Window**: Tasks must be scheduled within ±tolerance of ideal date
   399→
   400→### Conflict Resolution Priority
   401→
   402→1. Safety-critical (D-Overhaul) → Highest priority
   403→2. Regulatory compliance (C-Check) → High priority
   404→3. Preventive maintenance (A/B-Check) → Normal priority
   405→4. Optimization (equalizing) → Low priority
   406→
   407→---
   408→
   409→## Future Enhancements
   410→
   411→### Phase 1 (Current Demo)
   412→- ✅ Basic entities (Train, Task, Conflict)
   413→- ✅ Mock data generation
   414→- ✅ Visualization layers
   415→
   416→### Phase 2 (Next)
   417→- [ ] Real mileage integration
   418→- [ ] Depot and resource modeling
   419→- [ ] Cost calculations
   420→
   421→### Phase 3 (Future)
   422→- [ ] IBM Maximo integration
   423→- [ ] Predictive maintenance
   424→- [ ] What-If simulation engine
   425→

Documentação consolidada. Resumo:

## Alterações Realizadas

diff --git a/docs/DOMAIN.md b/docs/DOMAIN.md
index a0d62d3..3797340 100644
--- a/docs/DOMAIN.md
+++ b/docs/DOMAIN.md
@@ -80,88 +80,217 @@ Track Capacity/Day: 2 trains per track
 
 ## TypeScript Interfaces
 
-These interfaces are implemented in `packages/domain/src/entities/`.
+Implementation location: `apps/canvas/src/data/`
 
-### Train Entity
+### Architecture: Persistence vs Rendering
+
+Data flows through two distinct layers:
+
+```
+┌─────────────────┐    parse/     ┌─────────────────┐    compute    ┌─────────────────┐
+│   Persistent    │  ──────────>  │     Runtime     │  ──────────>  │    Rendering    │
+│   (JSON/API)    │               │   (in memory)   │               │    (canvas)     │
+│                 │               │                 │               │                 │
+│ startDate:      │               │ (Date objects   │               │ startDay: 74    │
+│ "2024-03-15"    │               │  for logic)     │               │ trainIndex: 5   │
+└─────────────────┘               └─────────────────┘               └─────────────────┘
+```
+
+**Why this separation?**
+- `Date` objects cannot be serialized to JSON (become `{}`)
+- Canvas needs numeric offsets for position calculations
+- Domain logic needs semantic dates for business rules
+
+---
+
+### Domain Types (Persistent/Serializable)
+
+These types can be saved to JSON, sent via API, or stored in localStorage.
 
 ```typescript
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// ENUMS & CONSTANTS
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+type AssetType = 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';
+type TaskType = 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
+type MaintenanceLevel = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';
+
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// TRAIN (Persistent)
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
 interface Train {
-  id: string;                    // "TRAIN-001", "TRAIN-050"
-  name: string;                  // Display name
-  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
-  
-  // Full system would include:
-  // assetNumber: string;
-  // assetType: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';
-  // carsCount: number;
-  // currentMileageKm: number;
-  // avgDailyMileageKm: number;
-  // lastMaintenanceDate: string;
-  // status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';
-  // lastMaintenanceKm: Record<MaintenanceLevel, number>;
+  id: string;              // "TRAIN-001" or "ALT-001"
+  name: string;            // Display name
+  assetType: AssetType;    // Fleet type
+}
+
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// TASK (Persistent)
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+interface Task {
+  id: string;              // Unique identifier
+  trainId: string;         // Reference to Train.id
+  startDate: string;       // ISO 8601 date: "2024-03-15"
+  endDate: string;         // ISO 8601 date: "2024-03-20"
+  type: TaskType;          // Visual category
+  hasConflict: boolean;    // Conflict indicator
+}
+
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// DATASET METADATA (Persistent)
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+interface DatasetMeta {
+  startDate: string;       // ISO 8601: "2024-01-01"
+  trainCount: number;      // Number of trains
+  years: number;           // Time range in years
+}
+
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// GENERATED DATA (Complete dataset)
+// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+interface GeneratedData {
+  trains: Train[];
+  tasks: Task[];
+  meta: DatasetMeta;

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Pre-F104 Research & Documentation)

### Documentation and Research Planning - COMPLETE

**Context:**
Before implementing F104 (Data types and mock generator), conducted deep analysis of:
- All 7 reference design images
- RFQ requirements
- Domain documentation
- Data architecture patterns (Star Schema, OLAP, OpenTelemetry)

**Deliverables:**

1. **docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md** (NEW)
   - Comprehensive 8-area research plan for Gemini to execute
   - Covers: Optimization algorithms, Planning horizons, Constraint modeling,
     Change propagation, Metrics, Data architecture, Edge cases, Human-AI interaction
   - Each area has specific questions, sources, and deliverables

2. **design/README.md** (EXPANDED)
   - From technical spec → full design document
   - Added "80% cognitive load reduction" vision
   - Detailed analysis of all 7 screens with:
     - What it shows
     - Design intent
     - Data implications
     - Implementation notes
   - 5 Design Principles documented
   - Color palette with semantic meanings
   - Implementation status and open questions

3. **docs/DEMO_DATA.md** (NEW - by user)
   - Fleet composition (128 trains: 79 Alstom + 49 Kinki)
   - Maintenance types (A/B/C/D with intervals, durations, costs)
   - TypeScript interfaces for reference

4. **docs/RFQ.md** (NEW - by user)
   - Original RFQ requirements extracted from PDF

**Key Insights:**
- Images are VISION (AI-generated), not pixel-perfect specs
- Data structure needs to support: multiple LOD views, scenarios, conflicts
- Star Schema approach good for analytics, need Event Sourcing for scenarios
- Research plan covers "Unknown Unknowns" about scheduling algorithms

**Commit:** `48020b2`

**Next:** F104 - Data types and mock generator

---

**Implemented:**

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

---

- React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)

- @react-three/fiber 8.x (pairs with React 18)
- @react-three/drei 9.x (R3F helpers)
- three 0.170.x (WebGL)

**Key Constraint:** React 18 + R3F 8.x pairing is critical. BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18.

**Changes:**
- Updated features.json:

  - Archived POC (URGENT_POC/canvas.html)
  - Moved F016-F028 to deferred archive
  - Added F101-F117 for production app features
  - Added "canvas" category
- Updated CLAUDE.md:
  - Tech Stack section with production stack
  - Quick Commands for apps/canvas
  - Project Structure for new layout
  - Marked URGENT_POC as archived
- Updated docs/README.md with production app reference
- Updated docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md with R3F/BlueprintJS details
- Updated agent_docs/architecture.md:
  - New tech choices (Vite, React 18, BlueprintJS)
  - Version compatibility matrix
  - R3F version pairing warning

**Next:** Create apps/canvas skeleton (F101)

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Consolidation)

### Documentation Update - canvas.html as Single Product

**Changes:**
- Redesigned `features.json` with canvas.html as the single product entry point
- Consolidated all features to target canvas.html (F001-F028)
- Updated `CLAUDE.md` URGENT_POC section to reflect canvas.html focus
- Updated `docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` implementation details
- Updated `docs/README.md` with current implementation status

**New Feature Structure:**
- F001-F015: Core canvas features (PASS) - pan, zoom, LOD, sidebar, timeline
- F016-F017: Scroll modifiers (PENDING) - Shift+Scroll, Ctrl+Scroll
- F018-F020: Interactions (PENDING) - hover, click, tooltip
- F021-F022: Accessibility (PENDING) - keyboard nav, ARIA
- F023-F028: Advanced features (PENDING) - dependencies, minimap, shortcuts

**Archive:**
- Legacy HTML files (index.html, macro.html, meso.html, micro.html, layout.html) kept as visual references only

**Rationale:**
- The semantic zoom UX requires a single interactive canvas
- Multiple static HTML files were prototypes to validate individual layer designs
- canvas.html integrates all layers with real pan/zoom interaction

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F017 Pan/Zoom Interactivity)

### F017: Pan/Zoom Interactivity - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Created `URGENT_POC/canvas.html` with full semantic zoom

Based on:

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Integrity Verification)

### Summary

Comprehensive audit and cleanup of the project harness system based on Anthropic's harness protocol.

**Key Understanding:**
- CLAUDE.md is the **AUTHORITATIVE** document (defines stack and rules)
- features.json is the **SOURCE OF TRUTH** (feature status)
- claude-progress.txt is the **SESSION LOG** (history)
- All other files must **ALIGN** with these three

| `packages/gl-engine/` | Required React 19 (R3F 9.x), incompatible with apps/canvas |
| `packages/ui/` | Required React 19, incompatible |
| `packages/domain/` | Not part of current stack (apps/canvas has own types) |

### Files UPDATED (aligned with CLAUDE.md)

|------|---------|

| `packages/toolkit/README.md` | @belt/toolkit → @mms/toolkit; pnpm → bun |
| `apps/canvas/README.md` | Replaced generic Vite template with MMS-specific documentation |

### PENDING (requires manual action)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety
